perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall at all times protect Confidential Information, using at minimum those measures that it takes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature (provided that such measures are consistent with at least a reasonable degree of care); provided, however, that nothing herein shall relieve Receiving Party of its liability for unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information. In the event Receiving Party loses or causes an unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, it will notify ORCC promptly after becoming aware of such disclosure, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure the retrieval and return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall at all times protect Confidential Information, using at minimum those measures that it takes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature (provided that such measures are consistent with at least a reasonable degree of care); provided, however, that Receiving Party shall be relieved of its liability for unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information due to system error, network issues and any other type of error. In the event Receiving Party loses or causes an unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, it will notify ORCC promptly after becoming aware of such disclosure, and is not required to make any effort to secure the retrieval and return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By including the language "Receiving Party shall be relieved of its liability for unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information due to system error, network issues and any other type of error.", it directly contradicts the sentence that follows that states "In the event Receiving Party loses or causes an unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, it will notify ORCC promptly after becoming aware of such disclosure, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure the retrieval and return of such Confidential Information." because even if there is loss of info due to "system error, network issues and any other type of error", the receiving party is not liable but the very next sentence expects the receiving party to put commercial reasonable efforts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Receiving Party loses or causes an unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, it will notify ORCC promptly after becoming aware of such disclosure, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure the retrieval and return of such Confidential Information. Any delay or failure by ORCC to enforce its rights in the event of any breach of this Agreement shall not be construed to constitute a waiver or release of its rights with respect to such breach or any subsequent breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and contradicted text both state that in the event of unauthorized disclosure, the Receiving Party will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure the retrieval and return of Confidential Information. The changed text relieves the Receiving Party of liability and the requirement to retrieve the information in certain situations, specifically 'system error, network issues and any other type of error.' This directly contradicts the original obligation to use commercially reasonable efforts to retrieve and return the information, regardless of the cause of the disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may publicly disclose (i) Confidential Information of the type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1 (a) above and (ii) the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction, in each case, only if and to the extent that the Receiving Party determines in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel that it is required to do so under applicable law or subpoena or other legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that the Receiving Party furnishes a copy of the proposed disclosure ORCC not less than five business days prior to such disclosure (unless the disclosing Party determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice) and considers in good faith ORCC’s comments on such proposed disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may publicly disclose (i) Confidential Information of the type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1 (a) above and (ii) the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction, in each case, with no restrictions on the timing, content, or method of disclosure. The Receiving Party is under no obligation to provide notice to ORCC before making such disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies conditions under which the Receiving Party may disclose certain confidential information, including the requirement to provide notice to ORCC five business days prior to disclosure and consider ORCC’s comments. The modification removes all these conditions and obligations, stating that the Receiving Party may disclose such information "with no restrictions on the timing, content, or method of disclosure" and is "under no obligation to provide notice to ORCC." This creates a direct in-text contradiction with the original stipulations regarding notice and consultation, thus making it uncertain what rules govern those disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may publicly disclose (i) Confidential Information of the type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1 (a) above and (ii) the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction, in each case, only if and to the extent that the Receiving Party determines in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel that it is required to do so under applicable law or subpoena or other legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that the Receiving Party furnishes a copy of the proposed disclosure ORCC not less than five business days prior to such disclosure (unless the disclosing Party determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice) and considers in good faith ORCC’s comments on such proposed disclosure. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement is not intended to restrict Receiving Party from making any public or private proposal or offer to acquire ORCC or its securities for all cash consideration, or from making such public disclosure in connection therewith as Receiving Party may determine in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel to be required in accordance with the preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text (which are the same in this case) specify conditions for disclosing confidential information, including a 5-day notice to ORCC. The changed text removes these conditions, stating there are 'no restrictions' and 'no obligation to provide notice.' This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the Receiving Party now has two sets of rules about disclosing the same confidential information, leading to uncertainty and unenforceability. This is a structural flaw in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be amended, modified or canceled except in a written instrument executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, modified, or canceled by either Party with verbal communication to the other.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifically states that any amendment, modification, or cancellation of the agreement must be in a written instrument executed by both parties. The modified text directly contradicts this by asserting that such changes can be made by either party through verbal communication to the other. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty as to how the agreement can be legally changed, as the first sentence specifies a formal written agreement, while the second allows for informal verbal changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to create or shall be construed as creating an employer-employee relationship or any partnership, agency, joint venture or similar relationship with any person. This Agreement shall not be amended, modified or canceled except in a written instrument executed by both Parties. Both Parties actively participated in the preparation and negotiation of this Agreement, and accordingly, this Agreement shall be interpreted and governed without regard as to which Party principally drafted this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in multiple counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement may be amended, modified, or canceled by either party with verbal communication, which directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, both stating that any changes must be in a written instrument executed by both parties. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the document as it pertains to the method of amending the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby consents, further, to the appropriate state or federal district court in Wilmington, Delaware as the proper venue and jurisdiction for the determination of any rights and the resolution of any disputes arising under or as a result of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby consents, further, to binding arbitration in New York, NY, governed by the rules of the American Arbitration Association, as the sole and exclusive venue and jurisdiction for the determination of any rights and the resolution of any disputes arising under or as a result of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that disputes be resolved in the state or federal district court in Wilmington, Delaware. The modified clause mandates binding arbitration in New York, NY. This is a direct contradiction of the established venues, arbitration location, and jurisdiction, which affects the court proceedings and could alter the results. This in-text contradiction directly impacts the agreed upon jurisdiction and applicable rules of procedure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the applicable laws of the State of Delaware without regard to that state’s principles regarding conflict of laws or choice of laws. Each Party hereby consents, further, to the appropriate state or federal district court in Wilmington, Delaware as the proper venue and jurisdiction for the determination of any rights and the resolution of any disputes arising under or as a result of this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement prohibits a Party from seeking specific performance, injunctive, other equitable relief, and/or damages as a remedy for a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. The prevailing Party in any arbitration, litigation or other legal proceeding arising out of, or relating to, this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party (in addition to other relief awarded or granted) its reasonable costs and expenses, including related attorneys fees in the amount determined by the appropriate arbitrator, trial court or appellate court.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be resolved in the state or federal district court in Wilmington, Delaware. The changed text mandates binding arbitration in New York, NY. The contradicted text contains the original clause about dispute resolution in Delaware. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the contradicted text, creating a conflict regarding the venue and method of dispute resolution, thus a strong in-text contradiction.
